query: Uganda Ebola Bundibugyo virus outbreak confirmed case count latest update late June and July 2026. Have any new cases been reported after 21 June 2026 bringing Uganda total above 20? Check ECDC CDTR Week 27, WHO Disease Outbreak News.
answer: Research complete. **Uganda's confirmed Bundibugyo Ebola case count remains at 20 and has NOT risen above 20.** No new cases have been reported after the last confirmed case on 21 June 2026, per ECDC (updated 10 July, citing CDTR Week 27), WHO DON612, and the WHO DON of 3 July 2026. Uganda has 2 deaths plus 1 probable case that died; 16 patients have recovered. The outbreak's rapid growth is confined to the DRC (1,792 confirmed / 625 deaths as of 8 July 2026).

sources.title: Ebola disease outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo and Uganda
sources.snippet: As of 9 July 2026, Uganda 20 confirmed cases and 2 deaths; last confirmed case reported 21 June, none since. References CDTR Week 27.

sources.title: Ebola disease caused by Bundibugyo virus, DRC & Uganda (DON612)
sources.snippet: 1481 confirmed cases; 1460 DRC, 20 Uganda, 1 France; 454 deaths including two from Uganda.

sources.title: Disease Outbreak News: Ebola caused by Bundibugyo virus, DRC & Uganda (3 July 2026)
sources.snippet: As of 2 July, Uganda has reported 20 confirmed cases including two deaths, plus one probable case who has died. Last confirmed case identified 21 June 2026.

sources.title: Bundibugyo virus disease updates - NICD
sources.snippet: As of 8 July 2026, 1792 laboratory-confirmed cases (including 625 deaths) reported in DRC provinces of Ituri, North Kivu and South Kivu.

sources.title: Ebola Map — Live Global Ebola Outbreak Tracker
sources.snippet: As of 8 July (DRC)/9 July (Uganda): DRC 1,792 confirmed/625 deaths; Uganda 20 confirmed/2 deaths; outbreak total 1,812 cases and 627 deaths.

sources.title: 2026 Ebola epidemic - Wikipedia
sources.snippet: Caused by Bundibugyo ebolavirus; declared PHEIC by WHO on 16 May 2026; two deaths in Uganda as of second week of July.

key_facts: Uganda has 20 confirmed Bundibugyo Ebola cases and has NOT exceeded 20 as of 9-10 July 2026.
No new confirmed cases have been reported in Uganda since the last case identified on 21 June 2026.
Uganda has 2 confirmed deaths (both imported cases) plus 1 probable case who died.
Of Uganda's 20 confirmed cases, 15 were imported (travel-linked to DRC) and 5 were secondary/local transmission (contacts and healthcare workers).
16 of Uganda's confirmed patients have recovered; cases confined to Kampala and Wakiso districts with no ongoing community transmission.
ECDC page (updated 10 July 2026) and CDTR Week 27 (27 June–3 July 2026) confirm Uganda = 20 confirmed, 2 deaths, no cases since 21 June.
WHO DON612 (published 10 July 2026) lists 20 Uganda cases; WHO DON of 3 July 2026 lists 20 confirmed + 2 deaths as of 2 July.
The outbreak's rapid growth is occurring in the DRC (1,792 confirmed / 625 deaths as of 8 July 2026), not Uganda.
Outbreak caused by Bundibugyo ebolavirus; declared a PHEIC by WHO on 16 May 2026.
